title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Detective Commander Klatdock arrives at Gossington Manor and meets Blackley Preston, an assistant to Jason Rad. Preston expresses the company's willingness to cooperate with the investigation and suggests that the deceased, Mrs. Badcock, may have died from an allergic reaction. However, Dr. Mortimer Cragdon clarifies that Mrs. Badcock was healthy and actually died from consuming six times the usual dose of camphor. Preston admits to using camphor himself to calm his emotions and notes that the drug is available in most medicine cabinets throughout the house.

As they move toward the staircase, Preston explains that he and Ella Jelinsky led the guests up the stairs in small batches. He recalls that Mrs. Badcock arrived with her husband, who wore a large official necklace, and Mrs. Bantry. Preston is unsure who served them drinks, noting that several people were responsible for service. He also mentions the presence of reporter Jim Galbreath, various photographers, and the unexpected arrival of Adwick Fenn, a prominent figure in the entertainment industry who has known Marina Gregg for years.

Klatdock observes that any of the thirty people present could have potentially poisoned Mrs. Badcock and inquires about the staff responsible for serving the drinks. When Klatdock requests to speak with Marina Gregg, Preston refuses, claiming she is in a state of deep depression and is under the care of a private physician. Klatdock then reviews a medical certificate regarding her condition provided by Preston.
